N+W is at rows 21-23, cols 9-11. The left door is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The door interior is at rows 33-35, cols 12-14.
I need to move N+W from rows 21-23, cols 9-11 to rows 33-35, cols 12-14.
That's DOWN x12 and RIGHT x3.
But wait, I've been going back and forth. Let me think about this more carefully.
The door is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The door interior is rows 33-35, cols 12-14.
N+W is at rows 21-23, cols 9-11.
To get to rows 33-35, cols 12-14:
- DOWN: 33 - 21 = 12 rows
- RIGHT: 12 - 9 = 3 cols
So I need DOWN x12 and RIGHT x3.
But wait, I already tried this approach and it didn't complete the level. Maybe the goal isn't to put both agents in doors. Let me reconsider.
Actually, looking at the scene again:
- There are two N squares with different center colors (W and c)
- There are two doors (charcoal frames)
- The N+c is already inside the right door
- The N+W needs to get into the left door
But maybe the goal isn't about doors at all. Maybe it's about matching the N square's center color to something else.
Let me look at the left door more carefully. The left door has a white interior (www). The N+W has a white center. So if the goal is to match center colors to door interiors, N+W should go into the left door.
But I've been trying to move N+W into the left door and it hasn't completed the level. Maybe there's something else I need to do.
Wait, let me reconsider the whole game. Maybe the goal is:
1. Move N+W to the left door (matching white center to white interior)
2. Move N+c to the right door (matching charcoal center to... what's the right door's interior?)
Let me check the right door's interior. The N+c is at rows 27-29, cols 45-47, inside the right door. The right door is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The interior would be rows 27-29, cols 45-47, which is where N+c is. So the interior color is... the N+c square itself, which has a charcoal center.
Actually, I think I need to reconsider. Maybe the goal is to have both N squares inside their respective doors, with the center color matching the door interior color. But the door interiors are white (www), so both doors have white interiors. That means both N squares need white centers. But one has a charcoal center.
Wait, maybe the doors have different interior colors. Let me check more carefully.
Actually, I realize I should look at the initial state of the doors before any N square entered. The left door at rows 32-36, cols 11-15 has interior at rows 33-35, cols 12-14, which is www (white). The right door at rows 26-30, cols 44-48 has interior at rows 27-29, cols 45-47, which is also www (white).
So both doors have white interiors. This means the goal might be to get both N squares (with white centers) into the doors. But one N square has a charcoal center.
Hmm, maybe I need to swap the centers again. Let me click on the N+c square to swap centers, making both N squares have white centers. Then both can enter the doors.
Wait, but when I clicked on N+c earlier, the centers swapped. So N+W became N+c and N+c became N+W. If I click on N+c again, they'd swap back.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The goal might be:
- N+W (white center) goes to the left door (white interior) ✓
- N+c (charcoal center) goes to the right door (charcoal interior?)
But the right door interior is white, not charcoal. Unless the interior color changes based on which N square enters.
Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me just try to get N+W into the left door and see what happens. From rows 21-23, cols 9-11 to rows 33-35, cols 12-14: DOWN x12, RIGHT x3.
But I've already used 62 actions, and the target is 28. I'm way over. Let me just try this and see if it completes the level.
